regimeSub: Sticky 4.2% inflation meets a hike-biased Warsh Fed even as US-Iran de-escalation drains the oil war premium.
themes: US-Iran road map collapses Hormuz risk premium -> Brent back to $78, energy CPI tailwind fades
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias and flags September hike -> front-end repriced higher, equities wobble
CPI at 4.2% three-year high with negative real policy rate -> Fed boxed into staying restrictive
Oil relief vs hawkish Fed cross-currents -> rate-hike odds, not geopolitics, now drive risk

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Warsh sees this as too loose, hike risk live.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +27bp, modestly positive; hike-bias flattening pressure as front-end reprices to Warsh hawkishness.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still contained with VIX at 17; oil de-escalation caps energy-name blowout risk for now.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.3 — calm despite hike odds and trapped tankers; market trusts the Iran road map holds.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $78, down hard on US-Iran progress; war premium unwinding, key disinflation swing factor.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,172 — holding near highs on negative real rates and Fed-credibility doubts, not war bid.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,537 weak won despite soft DXY 101; KOSPI 8,721 ripping on semis, BoK-Fed divergence in play.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: KOSPI/Nikkei records signal SOX strength; AI capex intact but vulnerable to higher-for-longer rates.

headline: Oil's war premium is bleeding out on a US-Iran deal, but a new hawkish Warsh Fed staring at 4.2% inflation is the real market driver as hike odds climb.
